query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Politic', 'Shrewd or prudent in practical matters', 'Scholars noted a striking politic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pollen', 'The fertilizing element of flowering plants', 'The essay highlights the pollen of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pollute', 'To make foul or unclean', 'Critics argued that the plan might pollute the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Poltroon', 'A wretched coward, craven', 'The essay highlights the poltroon of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polyarchy', 'Government in which power is invested in 3 or more persons', 'Scholars noted a striking polyarchy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polycracy', 'Government by many rulers, polyarchy', 'The essay highlights the polycracy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polygamy', 'The custom of having more than one spouse at the same time', 'Scholars noted a striking polygamy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polyglot', 'Able to speak or write several languages', 'The author hoped the new policy would polyglot tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polygon', 'A figure having three or more sides', 'The essay highlights the polygon of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polyhedron', 'A solid figure with many flat faces and straight edges', 'The essay highlights the polyhedron of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polysyllable', 'A word with more than two syllables', 'Scholars noted a striking polysyllable between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polytechnic', 'Offering instruction in a variety of technical subjects', 'Her polytechnic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Polytheism', 'The belief of the existence of many gods', 'Scholars noted a striking polytheism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pommel', 'A knob, as on the hilt of a sword', 'Scholars noted a striking pommel between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pomposity', 'The quality of being pompous, self-importance', 'Scholars noted a striking pomposity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pompous', 'Affectedly grand, solemn or self-important', 'The reviewer described the tone as pompous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ponder', 'To wonder, to think of deeply', 'The author hoped the new policy would ponder tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pontiff', 'Any high or chief priest', 'Scholars noted a striking pontiff between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pontificate', 'To preside as a bishop, especially at mass', 'Over time, the community learned to pontificate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Populace', 'The common people of a nation', 'Her populace became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Popular', 'Liked or supported by many people', 'The essay highlights the popular of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Populous', 'Full of residents, as a region, heavily populated', 'The passage presents a populous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Portend', 'To serve as a warning or omen', 'The author hoped the new policy would portend tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Portent', 'An indication or omen of something about to happen', 'Over time, the community learned to portent its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Portentous', 'Ominously significant, portending evil', 'What seemed portentous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Portfolio', 'A large flat case for carrying documents', 'Her portfolio became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Portly', 'Rather heavy or fat', 'She spoke portly, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Poseur', 'A person who attempts to impress others by assuming a manner', 'Over time, the community learned to poseur its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Posit', 'Put forward as a fact or basis', 'The essay highlights the posit of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Posse', 'A body or force armed with legal authority', 'Scholars noted a striking posse between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Possess', 'To have, to have ownership of', 'Critics argued that the plan might possess the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Possessor', 'One who possesses', 'Her possessor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Postdate', 'To date with a date in the future', 'The author hoped the new policy would postdate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Posterior', 'Located behind, or towards the rear of an object', 'The essay highlights the posterior of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Posterity', 'Succeeding or future generations collectively', 'Scholars noted a striking posterity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Postgraduate', 'Further studies after completing a degree', 'Over time, the community learned to postgraduate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Posthumous', 'Arising, occurring or continuing after one''s death', 'The reviewer described the tone as posthumous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Postscript', 'A paragraph or phrase added to a concluded letter', 'Over time, the community learned to postscript its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Postulate', 'Suggest as a starting assumption', 'Over time, the community learned to postulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potable', 'Good for drinking without fear of poisoning or disease', 'The passage presents a potable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potency', 'Strength', 'Scholars noted a striking potency between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potent', 'Powerful, mighty', 'The passage presents a potent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potentate', 'Monarch or ruler', 'Over time, the community learned to potentate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potential', 'Existing in possibility, not in actuality', 'Her potential became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potion', 'A drink or draft, especially a magical one', 'The essay highlights the potion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Potpourri', 'A mixture of dried petals of roses or other spices', 'Her potpourri became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pragmatic', 'Dealing with things sensibly and realistically', '“She took a pragmatic approach to solving the problem.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pragmatist', 'Practical person, realist', 'Scholars noted a striking pragmatist between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prate', 'To talk much and to little purpose, to babble', 'Critics argued that the plan might prate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Prattle', 'To talk incessantly and in a childish manner, to babble', 'Over time, the community learned to prattle its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
